source.title: Are We Forming Humans or Producing Bots? | Catholic Futurist

summary.title_hr: Formiramo li ljude ili proizvodimo robote? | Katolički futurist
summary.abstract_hr: Epizoda podcasta "Catholic Futurist" istražuje ulogu umjetne inteligencije u obrazovanju, s naglaskom na katoličku perspektivu. Otac Ambrose Criste i Benjamin Crockett raspravljaju o tome je li svrha obrazovanja formiranje ljudi ili proizvodnja "robota". Ističe se da je prava misija katoličkog obrazovanja spasenje duša, dok se kritizira utilitaristički model modernog školstva. Razgovor se dotiče potencijala AI-a kao alata za podršku učiteljima, ali i opasnosti koje donosi, naglašavajući nezamjenjivu ulogu ljudskog učitelja i važnost "screen-free" škola.
key_points: Prava svrha katoličkog obrazovanja je spasenje duša učenika, učitelja i administratora, a ne samo postizanje materijalnih ili karijernih ciljeva.
Umjetna inteligencija može biti koristan alat za kršćansku ljudsku formaciju, ali je treba odbaciti ako ometa taj cilj ili promiče mehanistički model obrazovanja.
Moderno zapadno obrazovanje, uključujući katoličke škole u Americi, usvojilo je utilitaristički model usmjeren na kapitalističku proizvodnju, što je nehumano i modelirano po vojnom formiranju.
AI se može koristiti kao pomoćni alat za pripremu nastavnika, stvaranje kurikuluma i prevođenje sadržaja, ali ne smije zamijeniti osobno obrazovanje u učionici.
Ljudski učitelji su nezamjenjivi jer obrazovanje zahtijeva ljudsko vodstvo koje vodi iz neznanja u znanje i pomaže u potrazi za istinom.
Algoritmi tehnologije, poput onih na Amazonu, mogu biti dizajnirani za iskorištavanje ranjivih skupina, što ukazuje na potrebu za razboritim korištenjem AI-a.
Nada za katoličko obrazovanje leži u "screen-free" školama, gdje se djeca odgajaju bez stalnog izlaganja ekranima, što ih bolje priprema za suočavanje s tehnološkim izazovima.
summary.title_en: Are we forming people or producing robots? | Catholic Futurist
summary.abstract_en: The episode of the podcast "Catholic Futurist" explores the role of artificial intelligence in education, with an emphasis on the Catholic perspective. Father Ambrose Criste and Benjamin Crockett discuss whether the purpose of education is the formation of people or the production of "robots". It is emphasized that the true mission of Catholic education is the salvation of souls, while the utilitarian model of modern schooling is criticized. The conversation touches upon the potential of AI as a tool for supporting teachers, but also the dangers it brings, emphasizing the irreplaceable role of the human teacher and the importance of "screen-free" schools.
key_points_en: The true purpose of Catholic education is the salvation of souls of students, teachers, and administrators, and not only the achievement of material or career goals.
Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool for Christian human formation, but it should be rejected if it hinders that goal or promotes a mechanistic model of education.
Modern Western education, including Catholic schools in Amerika, has adopted a utilitarian model focused on capitalist production, which is inhumane and modeled after military formation.
AI can be used as an auxiliary tool for teacher preparation, creating curriculum, and translating content, but it must not replace personal education in the classroom.
Human teachers are irreplaceable because education requires human guidance which leads from ignorance to knowledge and helps in the search for truth.
Technology algorithms, like those on Amazon, can be designed for the exploitation of vulnerable groups, which indicates the need for the prudent use of AI.
Hope for Catholic education lies in "screen-free" schools, where children are raised without constant exposure to screens, which prepares them better for facing technological challenges.
